release_review.summary: Workbench File Browser now treats roots, breadcrumb, toolbar, segmented controls, and overflow triggers as action surfaces so workbench hover keeps pointer cursors while real reading surfaces preserve text selection.
checked_surfaces: Workbench File Browser root rows, RO/RW toggles, breadcrumb segments, toolbar buttons, segmented controls, and overflow/menu triggers
Workbench text-selection projection keeps true reading surfaces selectable while excluding action surfaces
Workbench input routing keeps wheel ownership unchanged for selected widgets and explicit scroll viewports
File Browser interaction tests cover action-surface and text-selection separation
File Browser E2E hover coverage confirms pointer cursor behavior and no I-beam regression
Docs and design notes describe the action-surface versus text-selection boundary consistently
Desktop-managed machine presentation and startup report paths remain separated from human terminal UI
Runtime Service compatibility contract fields and protocol version remain unchanged
release_review.same_window_rationale: The release changes Workbench File Browser interaction classification and documentation only. It does not add, remove, or rename Runtime Service JSON fields, does not change the protocol version, and keeps Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, sys.ping, provider link, runtime-control, SSH runtime bootstrap, and startup report consumers on the existing compatibility window.
